title: The Month Claude Became a Platform
description: Figma's stock dipped on a Friday. Claude Design wasn't the story, it was a symptom.
outcomes: Identify the six Anthropic updates that redefined what "using Claude" means
Map each update to where it changes how Claude is used day-to-day
Adapt your team's workflow to use Claude as a workspace, not just a window

title: The "Zero-to-Slide" Workflow
description: The blank slide is officially dead. Turn a messy brain dump into a boardroom-ready deck before your coffee gets cold.
outcomes: Transform raw documents and scattered notes into high-fidelity structured decks
Convert a messy brain dump into a boardroom-ready presentation
Reuse the same repeatable workflow on any future deck

title: What OpenClaw built. How Cowork makes most of it safe.
description: OpenClaw set the architecture for autonomous agents. Cowork packages the same architecture but narrows two of the four primitives by design. This module maps both.

author.name: Anjali Rao
author.bio: Works on Claude Code. 8 yrs in developer tooling and ML infra.

outcomes: Build a working agent on OpenClaw's four primitives at full power
Configure Claude Cowork to run the same architecture without administering a stack
Apply a shared rubric to evaluate OpenClaw, Cowork, and any agent product shipping next

curriculum.title: Segment 2 · Claude Code vs Cursor vs Codex CLI
Segment 3 · Sub-agents and the review loop
Segment 4 · Where this breaks (and what to skip)
Hands-on Demo · Ship a sub-agent that opens a PR
What's your read? The cohort is already weighing in.

title: The Spreadsheet that Thinks
description: Stop fighting broken VLOOKUPs and hours of manual data entry. What if your spreadsheet could read your data and understand what you want to do with it?

author.name: Vikram Iyer
author.bio: Building autonomous workflows for finance ops. ex-Meta.

outcomes: Bring the power of LLMs directly into your spreadsheet grids
Replace broken VLOOKUPs and nested IFs with natural-language prompts
Automate the most painful parts of your weekly data work

curriculum.title: Segment 1 · Why 2026 is the year of computer use
Segment 2 · The four leading agent runtimes
Segment 4 · What still breaks on real sites
Hands-on Demo · A bounded computer-use task
What's your read? The cohort is already weighing in.

title: MCP in Production
description: Model Context Protocol moved from spec to standard. How to actually wire agents to your tools and data without rebuilding the connector layer for every model.

author.bio: Builds eval and tool-call infrastructure. Previously at OpenAI on RLHF.

outcomes: Stand up an MCP server in 30 minutes
Connect Claude, Cursor, and Goose to the same toolset

Decide which four MCPs are worth shipping first

title: Video, Practically
